title: (Location via IWM) Lancaster Memorial
location_name: (Location via IWM), IWM-Registered Bomber Crash Memorials (Peak/Pennines)
memorial_type: Upright limestone cairn with incised black tablet

external_narratives.summary: IWM War Memorials Register entry for Lancaster W4904 memorial. The limestone cairn commemorates the crew of a Lancaster bomber that crashed on 28 September 1943.
key_details: Upright limestone cairn with incised black tablet

external_narratives.summary: Lancaster W4904 from 1667 Heavy Conversion Unit crashed after encountering icing conditions during a Bullseye training flight from Faldingworth. The aircraft entered a steep unrecoverable dive and suffered structural break-up in mid-air, with wreckage scattered over two miles near Moor Monkton and Marston Moor.
key_details: Icing conditions caused loss of control
Aircraft broke up in air during steep dive
Wreckage over two-mile track near Moor Monkton
One crew member was American RCAF

external_narratives.summary: The Lancaster Memorial Project documents the cairn memorial which was rededicated in September 2023 in a new location, commemorating the crew who died in the W4904 crash.
